translations.idiomatic: He turned to Chen Ping and asked when the chaos would end.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「項王為人，恭敬愛人，士之廉節好禮者多歸之。

translations.literal: Ping said, "King Xiang as a man is respectful and loves people; knights of integrity who love ritual mostly go to him.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping said, "Xiang Yu is courteous and kind; men of honor flock to him.

sentences.zh: 至於行功賞爵邑，重之，士亦以此不附。

translations.literal: As for performing merit and granting rewards of noble rank and towns, he values them heavily; knights also for this reason do not attach.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he grudges every fief and medal, so able men drift away.

sentences.zh: 今大王嫚而少禮，士之廉節者不來；

translations.literal: Now the great king is insulting and short on ritual; knights of integrity do not come;
translations.idiomatic: You are rough and scorn ceremony, so the high-minded keep their distance;

sentences.zh: 然大王能饒人以爵邑，士之頑頓耆利無恥者亦多歸漢。

translations.literal: yet the great king is able liberally to give people noble rank and towns; knights who are stubborn, greedy for profit, and shameless also mostly return to Han.
translations.idiomatic: but you scatter titles and land with a free hand, and the ambitious flock to Han.

sentences.zh: 誠各去兩短，集兩長，天下指麾即定矣。

translations.literal: If each discards the two shortcomings and gathers the two strengths, pointing the finger and waving the hand the realm would be settled.
translations.idiomatic: Merge his virtues with your generosity and you could settle the map with a gesture.

sentences.zh: 然大王資侮人，不能得廉節之士。

translations.literal: Yet the great king by nature insults people and cannot obtain knights of integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Your own temper still drives the best men off."

sentences.zh: 顧楚有可亂者，彼項王骨鯁之臣亞父、鍾離眛、龍且、周殷之屬，不過數人耳。

translations.literal: Yet Chu has a weakness you can exploit: King Xiang's few truly loyal ministers—Yafu, Zhongli Mo, Long Ju, Zhou Yin, and their like—number only a handful.
translations.idiomatic: Still, Chu can be thrown into confusion. Xiang Yu leans on only a handful of iron-willed advisers—Fan Zeng, Zhongli Mo, Long Ju, Zhou Yin—and no others of their stamp.

sentences.zh: 大王能出捐數萬斤金，行反間，間其君臣，以疑其心，項王為人意忌信讒，必內相誅。

translations.literal: If the great king can spare several myriads of jin in gold, run a counterplot, drive a wedge between lord and ministers and sow doubt in their hearts—King Xiang is by nature jealous and ready to believe slander—they will surely turn on one another within his camp.
translations.idiomatic: Pour gold into a whispering campaign: set his captains at odds with their king. Xiang Yu is suspicious by nature and swallows every rumor—you can make him slaughter his own pillars.

sentences.zh: 漢因舉兵而攻之，破楚必矣。」

translations.literal: Han then raises troops and strikes; Chu will certainly be broken."
translations.idiomatic: Then you strike, and Chu will collapse."

sentences.zh: 漢王以為然，乃出黃金四萬斤予平，恣所為，不問出入。

translations.literal: The King of Han agreed, issued forty thousand jin of gold to Chen Ping, and let him spend it as he saw fit without accounting.
translations.idiomatic: The king approved, handed Chen Ping forty thousand jin of gold, and told him to spend it freely with no receipts.

sentences.zh: 平既多以金縱反間於楚軍，宣言諸將鍾離眛等為項王將，功多矣，然終不得列地而王，欲與漢為一，以滅項氏，分王其地。

translations.literal: Ping then used much gold to loose counter-espionage in the Chu army, proclaiming that generals such as Zhongli Mo, serving as King Xiang's generals with great merit, yet in the end could not obtain fiefs of land to be kings, wished to join with Han as one, to destroy the house of Xiang and divide his land to be kings.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping flooded Chu with gold and rumor: Zhongli Mo and the rest had won battle after battle for Xiang Yu yet never received a kingdom; they meant to defect to Han, wipe out the Xiangs, and carve up Chu among themselves.

sentences.zh: 項王果疑之，使使至漢。

translations.literal: King Xiang indeed suspected them and sent envoys to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu took the bait and sent envoys west to sound Han out.

sentences.zh: 漢為太牢之具，舉進，見楚使，即陽驚曰：「

translations.literal: Han prepared a grand sacrifice of three beasts, raised the dishes to serve; when they saw the Chu envoy they feigned alarm and said, "
translations.idiomatic: Han laid a feast of ox, sheep, and pig, carried the platters forward, then stared at the envoy from Chu and cried, "

sentences.zh: 以為亞父使，乃項王使也！」

translations.literal: We thought you were Yafu's envoy—it is King Xiang's envoy!"
translations.idiomatic: We assumed you came from Fan Zeng—only an envoy from Xiang Yu!"

sentences.zh: 復持去，以惡草具進楚使。

translations.literal: They took the feast away again and with coarse fare presented to the Chu envoy.
translations.idiomatic: They whisked the banquet away and fed him rough soldiers' rations instead.

sentences.zh: 使歸，具以報項王，果大疑亞父。

translations.literal: When the envoy returned he fully reported to King Xiang; he indeed greatly suspected Yafu.
translations.idiomatic: The man went home and repeated every insult; Xiang Yu's mistrust of Fan Zeng deepened overnight.

sentences.zh: 亞父欲急擊下滎陽城，項王不信，不肯聽亞父。

translations.literal: Yafu wished urgently to attack and take Yingyang city; King Xiang did not believe him and was unwilling to heed Yafu.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Zeng begged for an all-out assault on Yingyang; Xiang Yu no longer trusted him and refused the plan.

sentences.zh: 亞父聞項王疑之，乃大怒曰：「天下事大定矣，君王自為之！

translations.literal: When Yafu heard that King Xiang suspected him, he raged and said, "The affairs of the realm are settled; my lord may do as he pleases!
translations.idiomatic: When Fan Zeng learned he was under suspicion he shouted, "So be it—finish this war without me!

sentences.zh: 願乞骸骨歸！」

translations.literal: I beg to request my bones and return!"
translations.idiomatic: I ask leave to go home an old man!"

sentences.zh: 歸未至彭城，疽發背而死。

translations.literal: Before he reached Pengcheng on the return journey, a carbuncle broke out on his back and he died.
translations.idiomatic: He never reached Pengcheng—a boil on his back killed him on the road.

sentences.zh: 平乃夜出女子二千人滎陽東門，楚因擊之。

translations.literal: Ping then by night sent out two thousand women at Yingyang's east gate; Chu therefore struck at them.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping slipped two thousand women out the east gate of Yingyang as a decoy; Chu threw its whole force at the diversion.

sentences.zh: 平乃與漢王從城西門出去。

translations.literal: Ping then with the King of Han went out through the west city gate.
translations.idiomatic: In the confusion he shepherded the King of Han out the west gate.

sentences.zh: 遂入關，收聚兵而復東。

translations.literal: They then entered the passes, gathered troops, and again went east.
translations.idiomatic: They slipped into Guanzhong, rallied the army, and marched east once more.

sentences.zh: 明年，淮陰侯信破齊，自立為假齊王，使使言之漢王。

translations.literal: The next year Marquis Huaiyin Xin broke Qi, declared himself acting King of Qi, and sent envoys to report to the King of Han.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Han Xin destroyed Qi and proclaimed himself acting king of Qi, then sent messengers to announce it to Liu Bang.

sentences.zh: 漢王怒而罵，平躡漢王。

translations.literal: The King of Han was angry and cursed; Ping trod on the king's foot.
translations.idiomatic: The king began to curse; Chen Ping kicked his ankle under the table.

sentences.zh: 漢王寤，乃厚遇齊使，使張良往立信為齊王。

translations.literal: The King of Han awoke to the point; then he treated the Qi envoy generously and sent Zhang Liang to establish Xin as King of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang caught himself, feasted the envoys handsomely, and dispatched Zhang Liang to confirm Han Xin as king of Qi.

sentences.zh: 於是封平以戶牖鄉。

translations.literal: Thereupon he enfeoffed Ping with Huyou Township.
translations.idiomatic: For the stratagem he rewarded Chen Ping with the income of Huyou township.

sentences.zh: 用其計策，卒滅楚。

translations.literal: Using his schemes, in the end they extinguished Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Those intrigues, followed through, brought Chu down for good.

sentences.zh: 漢六年，人有上書告楚王韓信反。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Han someone submitted a memorial accusing King of Chu Han Xin of rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: In Han's sixth year a memorial reached the throne accusing Han Xin, king of Chu, of treason.

sentences.zh: 高帝問諸將，諸將曰：「亟發兵阬豎子耳。」

translations.literal: The High Emperor asked the generals; the generals said, "Quickly mobilize troops and bury that brat alive."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor polled his marshals; they roared, "Mobilize at once and exterminate the whelp."

sentences.zh: 高帝默然。

translations.literal: The High Emperor was silent.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said nothing.

sentences.zh: 以問平，平固辭謝，曰：「諸將云何？」

translations.literal: He asked Ping; Ping firmly declined and said, "What did the generals say?"
translations.idiomatic: He turned to Chen Ping, who bowed off the question: "What did the generals advise?"

sentences.zh: 上具告之。

translations.literal: The emperor fully told him.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang repeated their answer.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「人之上書言信反，人有聞知者乎？」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Among those who submitted the memorial saying Xin rebelled, is there anyone who has heard and knows?"
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping asked, "Has anyone actually seen evidence of rebellion?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「未有。」

translations.literal: He said, "There is not yet."
translations.idiomatic: "No one," said the king.

sentences.zh: 曰：「信知之乎？」

translations.literal: He said, "Does Xin know?"
translations.idiomatic: "Does Han Xin know about the memorial?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「弗知。」

translations.literal: He said, "He does not know."
translations.idiomatic: "He has no idea," said the king.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陛下兵精孰與楚？」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Your Majesty's troops in quality—how do they compare to Chu's?"
translations.idiomatic: "How does your army stack up against Chu's at its peak?" Chen Ping asked.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「不能過也。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "Cannot surpass."
translations.idiomatic: "We fall short," Liu Bang admitted.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陛下將用兵有能敵韓信者乎？」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Among Your Majesty's generals in employing troops, is there one who can match Han Xin?"
translations.idiomatic: "Which of your commanders can outfight Han Xin?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「莫及也。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "None reach him."
translations.idiomatic: "None of them," he said.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「今兵不如楚精，將弗及，而舉兵擊之，是趣之戰也，竊為陛下危之。」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Now your troops are not as refined as Chu's, your generals do not match him, yet to raise troops to strike him—this is to hurry him into battle; I privately fear for Your Majesty's peril."
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping said, "Then an open attack only drives him to revolt before you are ready—that would be suicide, sire."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「為之柰何？」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "What is to be done about it?"
translations.idiomatic: "What do you suggest?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「古者天子巡狩，會諸侯。

translations.literal: Ping said, "In antiquity the Son of Heaven toured on inspection and met the lords.
translations.idiomatic: "Ancient kings toured the realm and received their vassals in person.

sentences.zh: 南方有雲夢，陛下弟出偽游雲夢，會諸侯於陳。

translations.literal: South there is Yunmeng; Your Majesty need only issue forth feigning a tour of Yunmeng and meet the lords at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: South of the river lies the Yunmeng preserve—announce a pleasure hunt there and summon the lords to meet you at Chen.

sentences.zh: 陳，楚之西界，信聞天子以好出游，其勢必郊迎謁。

translations.literal: Chen is Chu's western border; when Xin hears the Son of Heaven goes out on a goodwill tour, by the situation he must go to the suburbs to welcome and pay court.
translations.idiomatic: Chen sits on Chu's western frontier; Han Xin will assume a peaceful imperial progress and come out to the road to greet you.

sentences.zh: 而陛下因禽之，特一力士之事耳。」

translations.literal: And Your Majesty thereby seizes him—it is merely the work of one strong man."
translations.idiomatic: One burly guard can bind him before his escort knows what happened."

sentences.zh: 高帝以為然，乃發使告諸侯會陳，「吾將南游雲夢」。

translations.literal: The High Emperor thought it right; then he dispatched envoys telling the lords to meet at Chen, "I will tour Yunmeng to the south."
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang agreed, sent word for the kings to assemble at Chen, and proclaimed a southern outing to Yunmeng.

sentences.zh: 上因隨以行。

translations.literal: The emperor thereby followed and went.
translations.idiomatic: He set out on the pretended tour.

sentences.zh: 行至陳，楚王信果郊迎道中。

translations.literal: When he reached Chen, King of Chu Xin indeed welcomed him in the suburbs on the road.
translations.idiomatic: At Chen, King Han Xin met him beyond the walls as expected.

sentences.zh: 高帝豫具武士，見信，即執縛之。

translations.literal: The High Emperor had beforehand prepared armed men; when he saw Xin, immediately he seized and bound him.
translations.idiomatic: Bodyguards hidden in the train seized and bound him on the spot.

sentences.zh: 語在信傳。

translations.literal: The account is in Xin's biography.
translations.idiomatic: The rest is told in Han Xin's memoir.

sentences.zh: 遂會諸侯於陳。

translations.literal: Thereupon he met the lords at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: He then held court with the feudal lords at Chen.

sentences.zh: 還至雒陽，與功臣剖符定封，封平為戶牖侯，世世勿絕。

translations.literal: Returning to Luoyang, with the merit ministers he split tallies fixing enfeoffments, enfeoffed Ping as Marquis of Huyou, generation after generation not to cease.
translations.idiomatic: Back in Luoyang he carved the tallies of enfeoffment and named Chen Ping Marquis of Huyou in perpetuity.

sentences.zh: 平辭曰：「此非臣之功也。」

translations.literal: Ping declined, saying, "This is not your servant's merit."
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping demurred: "I do not deserve the credit."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾用先生計謀，戰勝克敵，非功而何？」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "I used your, sir's, plans and strategies, won battles and overcame the enemy—if not merit, what?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied, "Your stratagems won my wars—if that is not merit, what is?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「非魏無知臣安得進？」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Without Wei Wuzhi how could your servant have advanced?"
translations.idiomatic: "Without Wei Wuzhi I would never have reached your presence," said Chen Ping.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「若子可謂不背本矣！」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "You may be called one who does not forget his roots!"
translations.idiomatic: "A man who remembers who lifted him up," Liu Bang said with approval.

sentences.zh: 乃復賞魏無知。

translations.literal: Thereupon he again rewarded Wei Wuzhi.
translations.idiomatic: He heaped fresh rewards on Wei Wuzhi as well.

sentences.zh: 其明年，平從擊韓王信於代。

translations.literal: The next year Ping followed to strike King Xin of Han (the king of Han named Xin) at Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Chen Ping accompanied the emperor on the campaign against King Xin of Han—the northern king whose fief was named Han—in Dai.

sentences.zh: 至平城，為匈奴圍，七日不得食。

translations.literal: When they reached Pingcheng, they were surrounded by the Xiongnu; seven days without food.
translations.idiomatic: At Pingcheng the Xiongnu ringed the Han camp and starved them for seven days.

sentences.zh: 高帝用平奇計，使單于閼氏解，圍以得開。

translations.literal: The High Emperor used Ping's strange plan, caused the Chanyu's chief wife to intercede, and the siege thereby opened.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping's secret stratagem moved the Chanyu's senior wife to plead for a lift of the siege.

sentences.zh: 高帝既出，其計祕，世莫得聞。

translations.literal: After the High Emperor escaped, the plan was kept secret; the world could not learn of it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor never revealed what was promised; the trick died with those who used it.

sentences.zh: 高帝南過曲逆，上其城，望室屋甚大，曰：「壯哉縣！

translations.literal: The High Emperor passed south through Quyu, ascended its wall, looked at the houses and halls very grand, and said, "Grand is this county!
translations.idiomatic: Marching south he climbed the wall of Quyu, stared at the mansions below, and cried, "What a magnificent district!

sentences.zh: 吾行天下，獨見雒陽與是耳。」

translations.literal: I have traveled the world; only Luoyang and this."
translations.idiomatic: In all my travels I have seen nothing to match it but Luoyang itself."

sentences.zh: 顧問御史：「曲逆戶口幾何？」

translations.literal: He turned and asked the imperial clerk, "How many households are registered in Quyu?"
translations.idiomatic: He asked his secretary for the census count.

sentences.zh: 對曰：「始秦時三萬餘戶，間者兵數起，多亡匿，今見五千餘戶。」

translations.literal: He answered, "At the start under Qin more than thirty thousand households; recently warfare rose several times, many fled and hid; now a little over five thousand households are recorded."
translations.idiomatic: The reply ran, "Under Qin it held thirty thousand households; war after war drove people into hiding—only five thousand remain on the rolls."

sentences.zh: 於是召御史，更封平為曲逆侯，盡食之，除前所食戶牖。

translations.literal: Thereupon he summoned the clerk, changed Ping's enfeoffment to Marquis of Quyu, gave him the full income of the district, and removed the former sustenance from Huyou.
translations.idiomatic: On the spot he transferred Chen Ping's patent to Quyu and granted him the entire district, revoking the old stipend from Huyou.

sentences.zh: 平自初從，至天下定後，常以護軍中尉從擊臧荼、陳豨、黥布。

translations.literal: From Ping's first following until after the realm was settled, he often as commandant-in-chief of the guard followed to strike Zang Tu, Chen Xi, and Qing Bu.
translations.idiomatic: From his first enlistment through the pacification of the empire Chen Ping served as chief commandant of the guard in the campaigns against Zang Tu, Chen Xi, and Ying Bu.

sentences.zh: 凡六出奇計，輒益邑封。

translations.literal: In all six times he produced strange plans, each time his fief was enlarged.
translations.idiomatic: Six times he devised a coup de main, and each time his fief grew.

sentences.zh: 奇計或頗祕，世莫得聞也。

translations.literal: Some of the strange plans were rather secret; the world could not learn of them.
translations.idiomatic: Several of those stratagems remain state secrets to this day.

sentences.zh: 高帝從擊布軍還，病創，徐行至長安。

translations.literal: The High Emperor returned from following to strike Bu's army, ill from a wound, and slowly traveled to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Wounded on the Qing Bu campaign, the emperor limped back to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 燕王盧綰反，上使樊噲以相國將兵擊之。

translations.literal: King Lu Wan of Yan rebelled; the emperor sent Fan Kuai as chancellor of state to lead troops against him.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Yan of Yan revolted, Liu Bang named Fan Kuai acting chancellor and sent him north with an army.

sentences.zh: 既行，人有短惡噲者。

translations.literal: After he had set out, someone slandered Fan Kuai.
translations.idiomatic: No sooner had Fan Kuai marched than a whisperer poisoned the emperor's ear against him.

sentences.zh: 高帝怒曰：「噲見吾病，乃幾我死也！」

translations.literal: The High Emperor angrily said, "Kuai sees my illness and thinks I am as good as dead!"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor snarled, "Fan Kuai thinks I am dying and means to seize power!"

sentences.zh: 用平計，召絳侯周勃受詔床下，曰：「平乘馳傳載勃代噲將，平至軍中即斬噲頭！」

translations.literal: Using Ping's plan, he summoned Marquis of Jiang Zhou Bo to receive the edict at the bedside, saying, "Ping shall ride post-horses carrying Bo to replace Kuai as general; when Ping reaches the army immediately cut off Kuai's head!"
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping's plan was to send Zhou Bo post-haste with orders to relieve Fan Kuai—and to behead him the moment they reached camp.

sentences.zh: 二人既受詔，馳傳未至軍，行計曰：「樊噲，帝之故人，功多，又呂后女弟呂須夫，有親且貴，帝以忿怒故欲斬之，即恐後悔。

translations.literal: The two men having received the edict, before they reached the army on the post-ride they deliberated, saying, "Fan Kuai is the emperor's old friend, his merit is great, and he is the husband of Empress Lü's younger sister Lü Xu—close kin and honored; the emperor because of anger wishes to kill him—we fear he will regret it afterward.
translations.idiomatic: En route they reconsidered: Fan Kuai was an old comrade, a major conqueror, and son-in-law to the Lü clan—kill him in haste and the emperor would surely regret it.

sentences.zh: 寧囚而致上，令上自誅之。」

translations.literal: Better imprison him and deliver him up, let the emperor himself execute him."
translations.idiomatic: Better bind him and haul him to the capital and let Liu Bang decide."

sentences.zh: 未至軍，為壇，以節召樊噲。

translations.literal: Before reaching the army they built an altar and with the imperial credentials summoned Fan Kuai.
translations.idiomatic: They halted short of the army, raised a small platform, and used the tally to summon Fan Kuai to audience.

sentences.zh: 噲受詔，即反接，載檻車詣長安，而令周勃代將兵定燕。

translations.literal: Kuai received the edict, was immediately bound hands behind back, loaded in a prison cart to Chang'an, while Zhou Bo was ordered to replace him in command to settle Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Kuai accepted the summons, was trussed and packed into a cage-cart for Chang'an, while Zhou Bo took command and finished the Yan campaign.

sentences.zh: 平行聞高帝崩，平恐呂后及呂須怒，乃馳傳先去。

translations.literal: Ping on the road heard the High Emperor had died; Ping feared Empress Lü and Lü Xu would be angry, therefore he galloped post-horses and went ahead first.
translations.idiomatic: Word of the emperor's death overtook Chen Ping on the road; fearing Lü Zhi and her sister, he raced ahead of the main party to the capital.

sentences.zh: 逢使者詔平與灌嬰屯於滎陽。

translations.literal: He met an envoy with an edict ordering Ping and Guan Ying to garrison at Yingyang.
translations.idiomatic: Couriers met him with orders to join Guan Ying in garrisoning Yingyang.

sentences.zh: 平受詔，立復馳至宮，哭殊悲，因奏事喪前。

translations.literal: Ping received the edict, immediately galloped again to the palace, wept extremely bitterly, and memorialized before the bier.
translations.idiomatic: He accepted the writ, then wheeled his horses straight to the palace, threw himself weeping on the coffin, and reported his mission before the corpse.

sentences.zh: 呂后哀之，曰：「君出休矣！」

translations.literal: Empress Lü pitied him and said, "Sir, go out and rest!"
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü was moved and told him to go home and rest.

sentences.zh: 平畏讒之就，因固請之，得宿衛中。

translations.literal: Ping feared slander would succeed; therefore he firmly begged to remain and obtained lodging on guard within.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping insisted on standing night watch inside the palace so no one could accuse him of disloyalty during the succession crisis.

sentences.zh: 太后乃以為郎中令，日傅教帝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager thereupon made him supervisor of the palace gentlemen, daily attending to instruct the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: She named him superintendent of the palace gentlemen and tutor to the boy emperor.

sentences.zh: 是後呂須讒乃不得行。

translations.literal: After this Lü Xu's slander could not proceed.
translations.idiomatic: That post shielded him from Lü Xu's malice.

sentences.zh: 樊噲至，即赦復爵邑。

translations.literal: When Fan Kuai arrived, immediately he was pardoned and restored to noble rank and estates.
translations.idiomatic: When Fan Kuai reached Chang'an he was pardoned and his titles restored.

sentences.zh: 王陵，沛人也。

translations.literal: Wang Ling was a man of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling came from Pei county.

sentences.zh: 始為縣豪，高祖微時兄事陵。

translations.literal: At first he was a county magnate; when Gaozu was lowly he treated Ling as an elder brother.
translations.idiomatic: He had been a local power broker; in Liu Bang's lean years Wang Ling was the man Liu honored as an elder.

sentences.zh: 及高祖起沛，入咸陽，陵亦聚黨數千人，居南陽，不肯從沛公。

translations.literal: When Gaozu rose at Pei and entered Xianyang, Ling also gathered a following of several thousand, dwelt at Nanyang, and was unwilling to follow the Lord of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Bang first rebelled Wang Ling raised thousands of his own and sat in Nanyang, refusing to join the Lord of Pei.

sentences.zh: 及漢王之還擊項籍，陵乃以兵屬漢。

translations.literal: When the King of Han returned to strike Xiang Ji, Ling then attached his troops to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Only when the King of Han turned east against Xiang Yu did Wang Ling bring his command under Han.

sentences.zh: 項羽取陵母置軍中，陵使至，則東鄉坐陵母，欲以招陵。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu took Ling's mother and placed her in the army; when Ling's envoy arrived, he seated Ling's mother facing east, wishing thereby to win Ling over.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu seized Wang Ling's mother and held her hostage; when Han envoys arrived he seated the old woman in the seat of honor to lure her son.

sentences.zh: 陵母既私送使者，泣曰：「願為老妾語陵，善事漢王。

translations.literal: Ling's mother then privately saw off the envoy, weeping, and said, "Tell Ling for this old woman: serve the King of Han well.
translations.idiomatic: She slipped out to the envoy in secret and wept, "Give my son this message: serve the King of Han faithfully.

sentences.zh: 漢王長者，毋以老妾故持二心。

translations.literal: The King of Han is a true gentleman; do not on my account hold two hearts.
translations.idiomatic: He is a man of generous mettle—do not waver because of your mother.

sentences.zh: 妾以死送使者。」

translations.literal: This old woman dies to speed the envoy on his way."
translations.idiomatic: I will buy you time with my life."

sentences.zh: 遂伏劍而死。

translations.literal: Thereupon she fell on the sword and died.
translations.idiomatic: She drew a dagger across her throat.

sentences.zh: 項王怒，亨陵母。

translations.literal: King Xiang was angry and boiled Ling's mother.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu flew into a rage and had her corpse boiled.

sentences.zh: 陵卒從漢王定天下。

translations.literal: Ling in the end followed the King of Han and settled the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling still finished the war at Liu Bang's side.

sentences.zh: 以善雍齒，雍齒，高祖之仇，陵又本無從漢之意，以故後封陵，為安國侯。

translations.literal: Because he was friendly with Yong Chi—and Yong Chi was Gaozu's enemy—and Ling moreover originally had no mind to follow Han—therefore later he enfeoffed Ling as Marquis of Anguo.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang resented his old ties to Yong Chi, Gaozu's bitter foe, and his reluctance to enlist early, so Wang Ling was among the last enfeoffed—as marquis of Anguo.

sentences.zh: 陵為人少文任氣，好直言。

translations.literal: Ling as a man had little polish, followed his temper, and loved straight speech.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling was blunt, proud, and plain-spoken—little given to literary polish.

sentences.zh: 為右丞相二歲，惠帝崩。

translations.literal: Having been right chancellor for two years, Emperor Hui died.
translations.idiomatic: He served two years as right chancellor before Emperor Hui died.

sentences.zh: 高后欲立諸呂為王，問陵。

translations.literal: The High Empress wished to establish various Lüs as kings and asked Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Lü meant to make her kinsmen kings and sounded Wang Ling out.

sentences.zh: 陵曰：「高皇帝刑白馬而盟曰：『非劉氏而王者，天下共擊之。』

translations.literal: Ling said, "The High Emperor killed a white horse and swore: 'Whoever is not of the Liu clan and becomes king—the world shall strike him together.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling answered, "The High Emperor swore over a white horse that any non-Liu who took the title of king would be attacked by the whole realm."

sentences.zh: 今王呂氏，非約也。」

translations.literal: Now to make kings of the Lü clan breaks that oath."
translations.idiomatic: Elevating the Lüs would violate that blood oath."

sentences.zh: 太后不說。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was displeased.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager's face darkened.

sentences.zh: 問丞相平及絳侯周勃等，皆曰：「高帝定天下，王子弟；

translations.literal: She asked Chancellor Chen Ping and Marquis of Jiang Zhou Bo and others; all said, "The High Emperor settled the realm and made kings of his sons and younger brothers;
translations.idiomatic: She polled Chen Ping and Zhou Bo: both said, "The High Emperor enfeoffed his own kin when he won the empire.

sentences.zh: 今太后稱制，欲王昆弟諸呂，無所不可。」

translations.literal: Now the empress dowager holds the regency and wishes to make kings of her brothers and the various Lüs—nothing is impermissible."
translations.idiomatic: Now the empress dowager rules in her own name and wants the Lü brothers made kings—who are we to object?"

sentences.zh: 太后喜。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was pleased.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager brightened at the answer.

sentences.zh: 罷朝，陵讓平、勃曰：「始與高帝唼血而盟，諸君不在邪？

translations.literal: When court was dismissed, Ling rebuked Ping and Bo, saying, "At the beginning we smeared blood with the High Emperor in covenant—were you gentlemen not there?
translations.idiomatic: After court Wang Ling cornered them: "We tasted blood with the High Emperor in the old oath—were you not there?

sentences.zh: 今高帝崩，太后女主，欲王呂氏，諸君縱欲阿意背約，何面目見高帝於地下乎！」

translations.literal: Now the High Emperor is dead; the empress dowager, a woman ruler, wishes to make kings of the Lü clan; you gentlemen even if you wish to flatter her intent and betray the covenant—with what face will you see the High Emperor underground?"
translations.idiomatic: Now he is gone and a woman means to make the Lüs kings of the realm. If you truckle to her and break the oath, what will you say to him in the grave?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「於面折廷爭，臣不如君；

translations.literal: Ping said, "In confronting face to face and disputing at court, your servant is not your match;
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping said, "In blunt argument on the palace steps I am no match for you.

sentences.zh: 全社稷，定劉氏後，君亦不如臣。」

translations.literal: In preserving the altars of soil and grain and settling the posterity of the Liu house, you are also not your servant's match."
translations.idiomatic: In saving the dynasty and securing the Liu line, you cannot match me."

sentences.zh: 陵無以應之。

translations.literal: Ling had nothing with which to answer him.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling had no reply.

sentences.zh: 於是呂太后欲廢陵，乃陽遷陵為帝太傅，實奪之相權。

translations.literal: Thereupon Empress Dowager Lü wished to remove Ling; then she falsely promoted Ling to grand tutor to the emperor, in reality stripping his chancellor authority.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü meant to be rid of him: she "promoted" him to grand tutor to the boy emperor, a hollow title that stripped him of real power.

sentences.zh: 陵怒，謝病免，杜門竟不朝請，十年而薨。

translations.literal: Ling was angry, declined on grounds of illness, shut his door and to the end did not attend court or make requests; after ten years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling resigned in a fury, pleaded illness, and never crossed his threshold for court again; he died ten years later.

sentences.zh: 陵之免，呂太后徙平為右丞相，以辟陽侯審食其為左丞相。

translations.literal: When Ling was removed, Empress Dowager Lü transferred Ping to right chancellor and made Marquis of Biyang Shen Yiji left chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: With Wang Ling gone, Lü Zhi moved Chen Ping to the right chancellorship and named Shen Yiji of Biyang as left chancellor.

sentences.zh: 食其亦沛人也。

translations.literal: Yiji was also a man of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Yiji was another Pei county man.

sentences.zh: 漢王之敗彭城西，楚取太上皇、呂后為質，食其以舍人侍呂后。

translations.literal: When the King of Han was defeated west of Pengcheng, Chu took the Grand Supreme Emperor and Empress Lü as hostages; Yiji as a retainer attended Empress Lü.
translations.idiomatic: After the rout at Pengcheng, when Xiang Yu held Liu Bang's father and wife hostage, Shen Yiji served as Empress Lü's household steward in captivity.

sentences.zh: 其後從破項籍為侯，幸於呂太后。

translations.literal: Afterward he followed in breaking Xiang Ji and became a marquis, favored by Empress Dowager Lü.
translations.idiomatic: He followed the Han cause through the fall of Xiang Yu, won a marquisate, and became the empress dowager's intimate.

sentences.zh: 及為相，不治，監宮中，如郎中令，公卿百官皆因決事。

translations.literal: When he became chancellor he did not administer affairs; he supervised within the palace like a supervisor of the palace gentlemen; the dukes and ministers and hundred officials all came through him to decide matters.
translations.idiomatic: As chancellor he ignored the ministries and ran the inner palace like a chamberlain; every edict passed through his hands.

sentences.zh: 呂須常以平前為高帝謀執樊噲，數讒平曰：「為丞相不治事，日飲醇酒，戲婦人。」

translations.literal: Lü Xu often because Ping earlier had plotted for the High Emperor to seize Fan Kuai, repeatedly slandered Ping, saying, "As chancellor he does not attend to affairs; daily he drinks fine wine and dallies with women."
translations.idiomatic: Lü Xu never forgave Chen Ping for plotting Fan Kuai's arrest; she kept whispering that he neglected his desk for wine and women.

sentences.zh: 平聞，日益甚。

translations.literal: When Ping heard it, he did so all the more.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping heard the gossip—and behaved even more dissolutely.

sentences.zh: 呂太后聞之，私喜。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Lü heard it, she was privately pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü was privately delighted: a drunken chancellor posed no threat.

sentences.zh: 面質呂須於平前，曰：「鄙語曰『兒婦人口不可用』，顧君與我何如耳，無畏呂須之譖。」

translations.literal: She confronted Lü Xu in Ping's presence, saying, "The vulgar saying goes, "The mouths of women and children are not to be used"—only look how you and I stand; do not fear Lü Xu's slander."
translations.idiomatic: She summoned Lü Xu before Chen Ping and snapped, "They say never trust women's gossip—between you and me there is no room for these whispers."

sentences.zh: 呂太后多立諸呂為王，平偽聽之。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Lü largely established various Lüs as kings; Ping pretended to obey.
translations.idiomatic: As she enfeoffed one Lü kinsman after another, Chen Ping bowed and assented.

sentences.zh: 及呂太后崩，平與太尉勃合謀，卒誅諸呂，立文帝，平本謀也。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Lü died, Ping with Grand Commandant Bo conspired together; in the end they executed the various Lüs and established Emperor Wen; Ping was the origin of the plot.
translations.idiomatic: When she died, Chen Ping and Grand Commandant Zhou Bo struck together, extirpated the Lü clan, and enthroned Emperor Wen—the plot was Chen Ping's design.

sentences.zh: 審食其免相，文帝立，舉以為相。

translations.literal: Shen Yiji was removed from chancellor; when Emperor Wen was established, he was raised to be chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Yiji lost his post; once Emperor Wen sat the throne, Chen Ping was raised to sole chancellor.

sentences.zh: 太尉勃親以兵誅呂氏，功多；

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Bo personally with troops executed the Lü clan; his merit was great;
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo personally led the troops that butchered the Lüs—his was the greater martial feat.

sentences.zh: 平欲讓勃位，乃謝病。

translations.literal: Ping wished to yield the post to Bo; therefore he declined on grounds of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping meant to cede the premiership to him and pleaded illness.

sentences.zh: 文帝初立，怪平病，問之。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen was first established, he wondered at Ping's illness and asked about it.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen noticed Chen Ping's sudden "illness" and asked what ailed him.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「高帝時，勃功不如臣；

translations.literal: Ping said, "In the High Emperor's time, Bo's merit was not as great as your servant's;
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping said, "Under the High Emperor my record outshone Zhou Bo's.

sentences.zh: 及誅諸呂，臣功亦不如勃。

translations.literal: When it came to executing the various Lüs, your servant's merit was also not as great as Bo's.
translations.idiomatic: In the coup against the Lüs he surpassed me.

sentences.zh: 願以相讓勃。」

translations.literal: I beg to yield the chancellorship to Bo."
translations.idiomatic: The right chancellorship should be his."

sentences.zh: 於是乃以太尉勃為右丞相，位第一；

translations.literal: Thereupon they made Grand Commandant Bo right chancellor, rank first;
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo was named right chancellor, first in precedence;

sentences.zh: 平徙為左丞相，位第二。

translations.literal: Ping was transferred to left chancellor, rank second.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping moved to the left, second in rank.

sentences.zh: 賜平金千斤，益封三千戶。

translations.literal: They granted Ping a thousand jin of gold and increased his fief by three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: The court also gave Chen Ping a thousand jin of gold and three thousand extra households.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，上益明習國家事，朝而問右丞相勃曰：「天下一歲決獄幾何？」

translations.literal: After a while the emperor increasingly understood state affairs; at court he asked right Chancellor Bo, "How many cases does the realm decide in a year?"
translations.idiomatic: Soon Emperor Wen began grilling his ministers at audience: "How many lawsuits does the empire hear in a year?" he asked Zhou Bo.

sentences.zh: 勃謝不知。

translations.literal: Bo apologized, not knowing.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo sweated and said he did not know.

sentences.zh: 問「天下錢穀一歲出入幾何？」

translations.literal: He asked, "How much money and grain enter and leave the realm in a year?"
translations.idiomatic: "What of the annual revenue in cash and grain?"

sentences.zh: 勃又謝不知。

translations.literal: Bo again apologized, not knowing.
translations.idiomatic: Again Zhou Bo could not answer.

sentences.zh: 汗出洽背，媿不能對。

translations.literal: Sweat ran soaking his back; he was ashamed and could not reply.
translations.idiomatic: Sweat soaked his tunic; he stood mute with shame.

sentences.zh: 上亦問左丞相平。

translations.literal: The emperor also asked left Chancellor Ping.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor turned to Chen Ping.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「各有主者。」

translations.literal: Ping said, "Each has its overseer."
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping said, "Each office has its specialist."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「主者為誰乎？」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "Who are the overseers?"
translations.idiomatic: "Who holds those portfolios?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陛下即問決獄，責廷尉；

translations.literal: Ping said, "If Your Majesty asks about deciding cases, hold the commandant of justice responsible;
translations.idiomatic: "Ask the commandant of justice for criminal dockets.

sentences.zh: 問錢穀，責治粟內史。」

translations.literal: if you ask about money and grain, hold the minister of agriculture responsible."
translations.idiomatic: Ask the minister of finance for revenue and expenditure."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「苟各有主者，而君所主何事也？」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "If each indeed has its overseer, what affairs does your lordship oversee?"
translations.idiomatic: "Then what is a chancellor for?" asked the emperor.

sentences.zh: 平謝曰：「主臣！

translations.literal: Ping apologized, saying, "Your majesty's subject!
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping kowtowed: "I tremble before you, sire—

sentences.zh: 陛下不知其駑下，使待罪宰相。

translations.literal: Your Majesty does not know that I am a mediocre inferior whom you ordered to bear the blame of chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: You placed a dullard in the highest post.

sentences.zh: 宰相者，上佐天子理陰陽，順四時，下遂萬物之宜，外填撫四夷諸侯，內親附百姓，使卿大夫各得任其職也。」

translations.literal: A chancellor is one who above assists the Son of Heaven in ordering yin and yang and harmonizing the four seasons, below follows the suitability of the myriad things, outwardly pacifies the four barbarians and the lords, inwardly draws close to the hundred surnames, and causes the ministers and grandees each to fulfill their duties."
translations.idiomatic: A chancellor tunes heaven and earth for the throne, steadies the seasons, tends the myriad things, soothes the barbarians and feudal lords, cherishes the common people, and lets every minister do his proper job."

sentences.zh: 上稱善。

translations.literal: The emperor praised it as good.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen praised the answer.

sentences.zh: 勃大慚，出而讓平曰：「君獨不素教我乎！」

translations.literal: Bo was greatly ashamed; going out he rebuked Ping, saying, "You alone did not teach me in advance!"
translations.idiomatic: Outside the hall Zhou Bo hissed, "Why did you not coach me?"

sentences.zh: 平笑曰：「君居其位，獨不知其任邪？

translations.literal: Ping smiled and said, "Sir, you occupy that position—do you alone not know its duties?
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping smiled: "You took the chair—did you not know what it required?

sentences.zh: 且陛下即問長安盜賊數，又欲彊對邪？」

translations.literal: Moreover if Your Majesty had asked how many robbers there are in Chang'an, would you also have wished to force an answer?"
translations.idiomatic: Had he asked how many thieves infest Chang'an, would you have invented a number?"

sentences.zh: 於是絳侯自知其能弗如平遠矣。

translations.literal: Thereupon Marquis of Jiang knew his ability was far short of Ping's.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo realized how far he fell short of Chen Ping.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，勃謝病請免相，而平顓為丞相。

translations.literal: After a while Bo declined on grounds of illness and requested to be relieved of chancellor, while Ping alone became chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Soon Zhou Bo resigned the right chancellorship; Chen Ping held the post alone.

sentences.zh: 孝文二年，平薨，諡曰獻侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Emperor Xiaowen, Ping died; his posthumous title was Marquis Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping died in the second year of Emperor Wen with the posthumous name "Marquis Xian" (Accomplished).

sentences.zh: 傳子至曾孫何，坐略人妻棄主。

translations.literal: It passed to sons down to great-grandson He, who sat guilty of seizing another man's wife and rejecting the lord.
translations.idiomatic: The marquisate descended to his great-grandson Chen He, who lost it for abducting another man's wife and other crimes.

sentences.zh: 王陵亦至玄孫，坐酎金國除。

translations.literal: Wang Ling also reached a great-great-grandson, who sat guilty of inferior sacrificial gold; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling's line ran to a descendant who forfeited his fief over "thin" ritual wine—impure gold offered at the imperial shrines.

sentences.zh: 辟陽侯食其免後三歲而為淮南王所殺，文帝令其子平嗣侯。

translations.literal: Marquis of Biyang Shent Ji, three years after being removed from chancellor, was killed by the King of Huainan; Emperor Wen ordered his son Ping to inherit the marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: Three years after leaving office Shen Yiji was murdered by the king of Huainan; Emperor Wen let his son inherit the Biyang title.

sentences.zh: 淄川王反，辟陽近淄川，平降之，國除。

translations.literal: The Prince of Zichuan rebelled; Biyang was near Zichuan; Ping surrendered to him; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: When the prince of Zichuan rose in revolt, Shen Ping—holding a fief on that border—went over to him and lost the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 始平曰：「我多陰謀，道家之所禁。

translations.literal: Chen Ping at first said, "I have used many covert plots—what the Daoist school forbids.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping once warned his house, "I built my career on dark stratagems—the very thing Daoists condemn.

sentences.zh: 吾世即廢，亦已矣，終不能復起，以吾多陰禍也。」

translations.literal: My generations will then be cut off—that is enough; in the end they cannot rise again, because I have many hidden calamities."
translations.idiomatic: My line will wither; it will not flourish again, for I have stored up too much hidden harm."

sentences.zh: 其後曾孫陳掌以衛氏親戚貴，願得續封，然終不得也。

translations.literal: Afterward great-grandson Chen Zhang, because he was noble as kin of the Wei clan, wished to obtain renewed enfeoffment, yet in the end could not.
translations.idiomatic: Later his descendant Chen Zhang, kinsman by marriage to the Wei family, begged to revive the title but never received it.

sentences.zh: 周勃，沛人。

translations.literal: Zhou Bo was a man of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo was a native of Pei.

sentences.zh: 其先卷人也，徙沛。

translations.literal: His ancestors were men of Juan; they moved to Pei.
translations.idiomatic: His forebears came from Juan and later settled in Pei.

sentences.zh: 勃以織薄曲為生，常以吹簫給喪事，材官引強。

translations.literal: Bo made his living weaving silken shrouds for funerals, often played the panpipes to supply funeral services, and was a strongman archer recruit.
translations.idiomatic: He wove thin silk for mourning garments, blew the reed-organ at village funerals, and served as a "strong bow" conscript.

sentences.zh: 高祖為沛公初起，勃以中涓從攻胡陵，下方與。

translations.literal: When Gaozu first rose as Lord of Pei, Bo as palace attendant followed in attacking Huling and took Fangyu.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Bang first raised the Lord of Pei banner, Zhou Bo joined as a palace attendant and helped storm Huling and Fangyu.

sentences.zh: 方與反，與戰，卻敵。

translations.literal: Fangyu rebelled; he fought with them and repelled the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: Fangyu rose in revolt; Zhou Bo fought and drove the enemy back.

sentences.zh: 攻豐。

translations.literal: He attacked Feng.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned against Feng.

sentences.zh: 擊秦軍碭東。

translations.literal: He struck Qin's army east of Dang.
translations.idiomatic: He hit Qin forces east of Dang commandery.

sentences.zh: 還軍留及蕭。

translations.literal: He returned the army to Liu and Xiao.
translations.idiomatic: He withdrew the column to Liu and Xiao.

sentences.zh: 復攻碭，破之。

translations.literal: Again he attacked Dang and broke it.
translations.idiomatic: He struck Dang again and shattered the garrison.

sentences.zh: 下下邑，先登。

translations.literal: He took Xiayi, first to mount the wall.
translations.idiomatic: At Xiayi he was first over the wall.

sentences.zh: 賜爵五大夫。

translations.literal: He was granted the noble rank of fifth grandee.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to fifth-rank grandee.

sentences.zh: 攻蘭、虞，取之。

translations.literal: He attacked Lan and Yu and took them.
translations.idiomatic: He seized Lan and Yu.

sentences.zh: 擊章邯車騎殿。

translations.literal: He struck Zhang Han's chariots and cavalry in the rear guard.
translations.idiomatic: He smashed the rear guard of Zhang Han's chariots and horse.

sentences.zh: 略定魏地。

translations.literal: He seized and settled Wei territory.
translations.idiomatic: He helped secure the Wei region.

sentences.zh: 攻轅戚、東嬢，以往至栗，取之。

translations.literal: He attacked Yuancheng and Dongmin, advancing as far as Li and taking them.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned through Yuancheng and Dongmin to Li and took each town.

sentences.zh: 攻齧桑，先登。

translations.literal: He attacked Niesang, first to mount.
translations.idiomatic: At Niesang he again led the escalade.

sentences.zh: 擊秦軍阿下，破之。

translations.literal: He struck Qin's army below A and broke it.
translations.idiomatic: He broke a Qin army below A.

sentences.zh: 追至濮陽，下蘄城。

translations.literal: He pursued to Puyang and took Qi city.
translations.idiomatic: He chased the foe to Puyang and captured Qi.

sentences.zh: 攻都關、定陶，襲取宛朐，得單父令。

translations.literal: He attacked Duguan and Dingtao, raided and took Yuanku, and captured the magistrate of Shanfu.
translations.idiomatic: He stormed Duguan and Dingtao, surprised Yuanku, and seized the magistrate of Shanfu.

sentences.zh: 夜襲取臨濟，攻壽張，以前至卷，破李由雍丘下。

translations.literal: By night he raided and took Linji, attacked Shouzhang, advanced as far as Juan, and below Yongqiu broke Li You.
translations.idiomatic: He took Linji by night, fought through Shouzhang to Juan, and below Yongqiu shattered Li You's host.

sentences.zh: 攻開封，先至城下為多。

translations.literal: He attacked Kaifeng, arriving first below the wall in greatest number.
translations.idiomatic: At Kaifeng his unit was first to the walls in strength.

sentences.zh: 後章邯破項梁，沛公與項羽引兵東如碭。

translations.literal: Afterward Zhang Han defeated Xiang Liang; the Lord of Pei with Xiang Yu led troops east toward Dang.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Han destroyed Xiang Liang, Liu Bang and Xiang Yu marched east toward Dang.

sentences.zh: 自初起沛還至碭，一歲二月。

translations.literal: From first rising at Pei to returning to Dang was one year and two months.
translations.idiomatic: From the first uprising at Pei to the return to Dang was fourteen months.

sentences.zh: 楚懷王封沛公號武安侯，為碭郡長。

translations.literal: King Huai of Chu enfeoffed the Lord of Pei with the title Marquis Martial-and-Peaceful and made him governor of Dang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai named Liu Bang Marquis Wu-an and governor of Dang commandery.

sentences.zh: 沛公拜勃為襄賁令。

translations.literal: The Lord of Pei appointed Bo magistrate of Xiangben.
translations.idiomatic: The Lord of Pei made Zhou Bo magistrate of Xiangben.

sentences.zh: 從沛公定魏地，攻東郡尉於成武，破之。

translations.literal: He followed the Lord of Pei in settling Wei territory, attacked the eastern commandant of Dong commandery at Chengwu, and broke him.
translations.idiomatic: He followed Liu Bang in the Wei campaigns, stormed the eastern commandant at Chengwu, and broke his line.

sentences.zh: 攻長社，先登。

translations.literal: He attacked Changshe, first to mount.
translations.idiomatic: At Changshe he was first on the walls.

sentences.zh: 攻潁陽、緱氏，絕河津。

translations.literal: He attacked Yingyang and Gou shi and cut the river fords.
translations.idiomatic: He took Yingyang and Gou shi and severed the Yellow River crossings.

sentences.zh: 擊趙賁軍尸北。

translations.literal: He struck Zhao Fen's army north of Shi.
translations.idiomatic: He hit Zhao Fen north of Shi.

sentences.zh: 南攻南陽守齮，破武關、嶢關。

translations.literal: Southward he attacked the governor of Nanyang named Yi, broke Wuguan and Yao Pass.
translations.idiomatic: He marched south against Nanyang's governor Yi and forced Wuguan and Yao Pass.

sentences.zh: 攻秦軍於藍田。

translations.literal: He attacked Qin's army at Lantian.
translations.idiomatic: He fought Qin at Lantian.

sentences.zh: 至咸陽，滅秦。

translations.literal: He reached Xianyang and extinguished Qin.
translations.idiomatic: He entered Xianyang and helped destroy Qin.

sentences.zh: 項羽至，以沛公為漢王。

translations.literal: When Xiang Yu arrived, he made the Lord of Pei King of Han.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu divided the realm he named Liu Bang king of Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王賜勃爵為威武侯。

translations.literal: The King of Han granted Bo the noble rank of Marquis Weiwu.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han ennobled him as Marquis Weiwu.

sentences.zh: 從入漢中，拜為將軍。

translations.literal: He followed in entering Hanzhong and was appointed general.
translations.idiomatic: He followed into Hanzhong and was commissioned a general.

sentences.zh: 還定三秦，賜食邑懷德。

translations.literal: In returning to settle the Three Qin he was granted sustenance at Huaide.
translations.idiomatic: In the reconquest of the Three Qin he received the income of Huaide.

sentences.zh: 攻槐里、好畤，最。

translations.literal: He attacked Huaili and Haozhi, rated highest.
translations.idiomatic: At Huaili and Haozhi his unit topped the battle roll.

sentences.zh: 北擊趙賁、內史保於咸陽，最。

translations.literal: Northward he struck Zhao Fen and Grand Secretary Bao at Xianyang, rated highest.
translations.idiomatic: North of Xianyang he routed Zhao Fen and Neishi Bao—again first in merit.

sentences.zh: 北救漆。

translations.literal: Northward he rescued Qi.
translations.idiomatic: He marched north to relieve Qi (the city).

sentences.zh: 擊章平、姚卬軍。

translations.literal: He struck the armies of Zhang Ping and Yao Ang.
translations.idiomatic: He fought Zhang Ping and Yao Ang.

sentences.zh: 西定汧還下郿、頻陽。

translations.literal: Westward he settled Qian and in returning took Mei and Pinyang.
translations.idiomatic: He secured the west in Qian, then doubled back to take Mei and Pinyang.

sentences.zh: 圍章邯廢丘，破之。

translations.literal: He besieged Zhang Han at Feiqiu and broke him.
translations.idiomatic: He besieged Zhang Han in Feiqiu and stormed the city.

sentences.zh: 西擊益已軍，破之。

translations.literal: Westward he struck the army of Yi Ji and broke it.
translations.idiomatic: He marched west and routed the detachment commanded by Yi Ji.

sentences.zh: 攻上邽。

translations.literal: He attacked Shanggui.
translations.idiomatic: He took Shanggui.

sentences.zh: 東守嶢關。

translations.literal: Eastward he guarded Yao Pass.
translations.idiomatic: He held the east approach at Yao Pass.

sentences.zh: 擊項籍。

translations.literal: He struck Xiang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: He fought Xiang Yu in the field.

sentences.zh: 攻曲遇，最。

translations.literal: He attacked Quyu, rated highest.
translations.idiomatic: At Quyu his command again led the honor roll.

sentences.zh: 還守敖倉，追籍。

translations.literal: He returned to guard Aocang and pursued Ji.
translations.idiomatic: He garrisoned Aocang, then rode in pursuit of Xiang Yu.

sentences.zh: 籍已死，因東定楚地泗水、東海郡，凡得二十二縣。

translations.literal: After Ji had died, he thereupon eastward settled Chu territory in Sishui and Donghai commanderies; in all he obtained twenty-two counties.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu fell he swept east through Sishui and Donghai commanderies, adding twenty-two counties to Han.

sentences.zh: 還守雒陽、櫟陽，賜與潁陰侯共食鍾離。

translations.literal: He returned to guard Luoyang and Liyang and was granted to share the sustenance of Zhongli with Marquis Yingyin.
translations.idiomatic: He garrisoned Luoyang and Liyang and was given a share of the Zhongli estate with Marquis Yingyin.

sentences.zh: 以將軍從高祖擊燕王臧荼，破之易下。

translations.literal: As general he followed the High Ancestor in striking King of Yan Zang Tu and broke him below Yi.
translations.idiomatic: As a general he joined the campaign against Zang Tu of Yan and broke his line at Yi.

sentences.zh: 所將卒當馳道為多。

translations.literal: The troops he led blocking the imperial thoroughfare were the most numerous.
translations.idiomatic: His men held the imperial highway in greater strength than any other unit.

sentences.zh: 賜爵列侯，剖符世世不絕。

translations.literal: He was granted the noble rank of full marquis, split tallies, generation after generation not ceasing.
translations.idiomatic: He was enfeoffed a full marquis with hereditary patent.

sentences.zh: 食絳八千二百八十戶。

translations.literal: He received sustenance at Jiang for eight thousand two hundred eighty households.
translations.idiomatic: His fief at Jiang numbered eight thousand two hundred eighty households.

sentences.zh: 以將軍從高帝擊韓王信於代，降下霍人。

translations.literal: As general he followed the High Emperor in striking King Xin of Han at Dai and took Huoren by surrender.
translations.idiomatic: He followed the emperor against King Xin of Han in Dai and received the surrender of Huoren.

sentences.zh: 以前至武泉，擊胡騎，破之武泉北。

translations.literal: Advancing first to Wuquan, he struck Hu cavalry and broke them north of Wuquan.
translations.idiomatic: He pushed to Wuquan and routed Xiongnu horse north of the town.

sentences.zh: 轉攻韓信軍銅鞮，破之。

translations.literal: He turned to attack Han Xin's army at Tongdi and broke it.
translations.idiomatic: He swung against Han Xin's host at Tongdi and crushed it.

sentences.zh: 還，降太原六城。

translations.literal: Returning, he reduced six cities of Taiyuan by surrender.
translations.idiomatic: On the way back he accepted the capitulation of six cities in Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 擊韓信胡騎晉陽下，破之，下晉陽。

translations.literal: He struck Han Xin's Hu cavalry below Jinyang, broke them, and took Jinyang.
translations.idiomatic: He smashed Han Xin's Xiongnu auxiliaries below Jinyang and took the city.

sentences.zh: 後擊韓信軍於硰石，破之，追北八十里。

translations.literal: Afterward he struck Han Xin's army at Shashi, broke them, and pursued the fleeing enemy eighty li.
translations.idiomatic: Later he shattered Han Xin again at Shashi and chased the fugitives eighty li.

sentences.zh: 還攻樓煩三城，因擊胡騎平城下，所將卒當馳道為多。

translations.literal: Returning he attacked three cities of Loufan, then struck Hu cavalry below Pingcheng; the troops he led blocking the thoroughfare were the most numerous.
translations.idiomatic: He doubled back to take three Loufan towns, then hit Xiongnu riders below Pingcheng with the heaviest screen on the highway.

sentences.zh: 勃遷為太尉。

translations.literal: Bo was promoted to grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo was raised to grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 陳豨，屠馬邑。

translations.literal: Chen Xi rebelled; he sacked Mayi.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Xi rose in revolt, Zhou Bo stormed and overran Mayi.

sentences.zh: 所將卒斬豨將軍乘馬降。

translations.literal: The troops he led beheaded Xi's general Chengma and took his surrender.
translations.idiomatic: His men cut down Chen Xi's general Chengma and accepted his surrender.

sentences.zh: 轉擊韓信、陳豨、趙利軍於樓煩，破之。

translations.literal: He turned to strike the armies of Han Xin, Chen Xi, and Zhao Li at Loufan and broke them.
translations.idiomatic: He pivoted to Loufan and broke the combined forces of Han Xin, Chen Xi, and Zhao Li.

sentences.zh: 得豨將宋最、鴈門守圂。

translations.literal: He obtained Xi's generals Song Zui and the governor of Yanmen named Hun.
translations.idiomatic: He captured Song Zui and the Yanmen governor surnamed Hun.

sentences.zh: 因轉攻得雲中守遫、丞相箕肄、將軍博。

translations.literal: He thereupon turned the attack and obtained the governor of Yun named Su, chancellor Ji Yi, and general Bo.
translations.idiomatic: He then seized Yun's governor Su, Chancellor Ji Yi, and General Bo.

sentences.zh: 定鴈門郡十七縣，雲中郡十二縣。

translations.literal: He settled seventeen counties of Yanmen commandery and twelve of Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: He pacified seventeen Yanmen counties and twelve in Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 因復擊豨靈丘，破之，斬豨丞相程縱、將軍陳武、都尉高肄。

translations.literal: He again struck Xi at Lingqiu, broke him, and beheaded Xi's chancellor Cheng Zong, general Chen Wu, and commandant Gao Yi.
translations.idiomatic: He struck Chen Xi again at Lingqiu, executed his chancellor Cheng Zong, general Chen Wu, and commandant Gao Yi.

sentences.zh: 定代郡九縣。

translations.literal: He settled nine counties of Dai commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He secured nine counties of Dai.

sentences.zh: 燕王盧綰反，勃以相國代樊噲將，擊下薊，得綰大將抵、丞相偃、守陘、太尉弱、御史大夫施屠渾都。

translations.literal: King Lu Wan of Yan rebelled; Bo as chancellor of state replaced Fan Kuai as general, struck and took Ji, and captured Lu Wan's great general Di, chancellor Yan, defender of Xing pass, grand commandant Ruo, and imperial secretary Shitu Hundu.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Yan revolted, Zhou Bo replaced Fan Kuai as acting chancellor, stormed Ji, and took Lu Yan's chief generals and ministers, including his secretary Shitu Hundu.

sentences.zh: 破綰軍上蘭，後擊綰軍沮陽。

translations.literal: He broke Lu Wan's army at Shanglan and afterward struck Lu Wan's army at Juyang.
translations.idiomatic: He defeated Lu Yan at Shanglan, then again at Juyang.

sentences.zh: 追至長城，定上谷十二縣，右北平十六縣，遼東二十九縣，漁陽二十二縣。

translations.literal: He pursued to the Great Wall, settled twelve counties of Shanggu, sixteen of Youbeiping, twenty-nine of Liaodong, and twenty-two of Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: He chased the king to the Wall and brought twelve Shanggu counties, sixteen in Youbeiping, twenty-nine in Liaodong, and twenty-two in Yuyang under Han.

sentences.zh: 最從高帝得相國一人，丞相二人，將軍、二千石各三人；

translations.literal: In all, following the High Emperor he obtained one man of chancellor of state rank, two chancellors, three each of generals and two-thousand-bushel officials;
translations.idiomatic: In the emperor's train he had captured one chancellor of state, two chancellors, and three generals and salary-class officials each;

sentences.zh: 別破軍二，下城三，定郡五，縣七十九，得丞相、大將各一人。

translations.literal: Separately he broke two armies, took three cities, settled five commanderies, seventy-nine counties, and obtained one each of chancellor and grand general.
translations.idiomatic: on his own he had shattered two armies, stormed three cities, pacified five commanderies and seventy-nine counties, and taken one chancellor and one grand general alive.

sentences.zh: 勃為人木強敦厚，高帝以為可屬大事。

translations.literal: Bo as a man was wooden, stubborn, and honest; the High Emperor thought him fit to entrust with great affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo was blunt, stubborn, and stolid—exactly the man Liu Bang trusted for the gravest duties.

sentences.zh: 勃不好文學，每召諸生說士，東鄉坐責之：「趣為我語。」

translations.literal: Bo did not love literary learning; whenever he summoned various scholars and persuaders, he sat facing east and rebuked them: "Hurry and speak plainly for me."
translations.idiomatic: He had no patience for scholars: when he had to hear them out he faced east like a judge and barked, "Make it short."

sentences.zh: 其椎少文如此。

translations.literal: His blunt lack of polish was like this.
translations.idiomatic: That was the measure of his rustic temper.

sentences.zh: 勃既定燕而歸，高帝已崩矣，以列侯事惠帝。

translations.literal: After Bo had settled Yan and returned, the High Emperor had already died; as a full marquis he served Emperor Hui.
translations.idiomatic: When he returned from the Yan campaign the High Emperor was dead; he served Emperor Hui as a marquis in good standing.

sentences.zh: 惠帝六年，置太尉官，以勃為太尉。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Emperor Hui the office of grand commandant was established and Bo was made grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Hui's sixth year the throne revived the grand commandant's office and gave it to Zhou Bo.

sentences.zh: 十年，高后崩。

translations.literal: In the tenth year the High Empress died.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year Empress Dowager Lü died.

sentences.zh: 呂祿以趙王為漢上將軍，呂產以呂王為相國，秉權，欲危劉氏。

translations.literal: Lü Lu took the title King of Zhao as Han's senior general; Lü Chan took the title King of Lü as chancellor of state; they grasped power and wished to endanger the Liu house.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Lu held Zhao as king while commanding Han's upper armies; Lü Chan was king of Lü and chancellor of state—they meant to unseat the Lius.

sentences.zh: 勃與丞相平、朱虛侯章共誅諸呂。

translations.literal: Bo with Chancellor Ping and Marquis of Zhuxu Zhang jointly executed the various Lüs.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo joined Chen Ping and Liu Zhang of Zhuxu to extirpate the Lü clan.

sentences.zh: 語在高后紀。

translations.literal: The account is in the Annals of the High Empress.
translations.idiomatic: The coup is narrated in the annals of Empress Gao.

sentences.zh: 於是陰謀乃為「少帝及濟川、淮陽、恆山王皆非惠帝子，呂太后以計詐名它人子，殺其母，養之後宮，令孝惠子之，立以為後，用彊呂氏。

translations.literal: Thereupon the secret plot became: "The Young Emperor and the kings of Jichuan, Huaiyang, and Hengshan are not Emperor Hui's sons; Empress Dowager Lü by a ruse named other men's sons, killed their mothers, reared them in the rear palace, ordered Emperor Xiaohui to treat them as sons, established them as successors, thereby to strengthen the Lü clan.
translations.idiomatic: The conspirators spread word that the boy on the throne and the princes of Jichuan, Huaiyang, and Hengshan were not Emperor Hui's sons at all—Lü Zhi had murdered their mothers, smuggled in strangers' children, and raised them in the harem so Hui would call them sons and the Lüs could tighten their grip.

sentences.zh: 今已滅諸呂，少帝即長用事，吾屬類無矣，不如視諸侯賢者立之。」

translations.literal: Now that we have already destroyed the various Lüs, when the Young Emperor grows and takes power, our sort will be finished; better to look among the lords for a worthy and establish him."
translations.idiomatic: Now that the Lüs are gone, those boys will grow up and wipe us out—we must pick a worthy Liu from among the feudal kings."

sentences.zh: 遂迎立代王，是為孝文皇帝。

translations.literal: Thereupon they welcomed and established the King of Dai, who is Emperor Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: They fetched the King of Dai to Chang'an and enthroned him as Emperor Wen.

sentences.zh: 東牟侯興居，朱虛侯章弟也，曰：「誅諸呂，臣無功，請得除宮。」

translations.literal: Marquis of Dongmou Xingju, younger brother of Marquis of Zhuxu Zhang, said, "In executing the various Lüs I had no merit; I beg to be allowed to clear the palace."
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xingju of Dongmou, Liu Zhang's brother, said, "I took no part in killing the Lüs—let me purge the palace instead."

sentences.zh: 乃與太僕汝陰滕公入宮。

translations.literal: Thereupon with Grand Coachman Lord Teng of Ru Yin he entered the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He entered with Grand Coachman Xiahou Ying of Ru Yin.

sentences.zh: 滕公前謂少帝曰：「足下非劉氏，不當立。」

translations.literal: Lord Teng stepped forward and said to the Young Emperor, "Your feet are not of the Liu clan; you ought not to be established."
translations.idiomatic: Xiahou Ying told the boy emperor, "You are no son of Liu—you cannot remain on the throne."

sentences.zh: 乃顧麾左右執戟，皆仆兵罷。

translations.literal: He then turned and signaled the left and right halberdiers; all cast down weapons and stood down.
translations.idiomatic: He signaled the guards; they grounded their halberds and stood down.

sentences.zh: 有數人不肯去，官者令張釋諭告，亦去。

translations.literal: Several men would not leave; Director of Attendants Zhang Shi instructed them and they also left.
translations.idiomatic: A few refused until Director of Attendants Zhang Shi explained the orders; then they withdrew.

sentences.zh: 滕公召乘輿車載少帝出。

translations.literal: Lord Teng summoned the imperial carriage and loaded the Young Emperor out.
translations.idiomatic: Xiahou Ying called up the imperial carriage and bundled the boy out of the hall.

sentences.zh: 少帝曰：「欲持我安之乎？」

translations.literal: The Young Emperor said, "You wish to take me—where?"
translations.idiomatic: The child cried, "Where are you taking me?"

sentences.zh: 滕公曰：「就舍少府。」

translations.literal: Lord Teng said, "Go to the lodging of the privy treasurer."
translations.idiomatic: "To the privy treasurer's quarters for now," Xiahou Ying replied.

sentences.zh: 乃奉天子法駕，迎皇帝代邸，報曰：「宮謹除。」

translations.literal: He then took the Son of Heaven's law chariot and welcomed the emperor at the Dai prince's lodging, reporting, "The palace has been respectfully cleared."
translations.idiomatic: He then drove the full imperial equipage to the heir's lodge in the city and announced, "The palace is swept clean."

sentences.zh: 皇帝入未央宮，有謁者十人持戟衛端門，曰：「天子在也，足下何為者？」

translations.literal: The emperor entered Weiyang Palace; ten ushers with halberds guarded the Duan Gate and said, "The Son of Heaven is within—what are you doing here?"
translations.idiomatic: At Weiyang Palace ten ushers blocked the inner gate: "The emperor is within—who goes there?"

sentences.zh: 不得入。

translations.literal: He could not enter.
translations.idiomatic: The new emperor could not pass.

sentences.zh: 太尉往喻，乃引兵去，皇帝遂入。

translations.literal: The grand commandant went to explain; then they led the troops away and the emperor thereupon entered.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo rode up to explain; the guards stood aside and Emperor Wen entered.

sentences.zh: 是夜，有司分部誅濟川、淮陽、常山王及少帝於邸。

translations.literal: That night the responsible officials in divisions executed the kings of Jichuan, Huaiyang, and Changshan and the Young Emperor at their lodgings.
translations.idiomatic: That night officials fanned out to kill the puppet princes of Jichuan, Huaiyang, Changshan, and the boy emperor in their residences.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，每河東守尉行縣至絳，絳侯勃自畏恐誅，常被甲，令家人持兵以見。

translations.literal: After more than a year, whenever the governor and commandant of Hedong toured the counties and reached Jiang, Marquis of Jiang Bo himself feared execution and was terrified; he always wore armor and ordered his household to bear weapons when meeting them.
translations.idiomatic: For over a year, whenever Hedong's governor inspected Jiang county, Zhou Bo went in armor and had his family carry arms to the meeting—he was terrified of a death warrant.

sentences.zh: 其後人有上書告勃欲反，下廷尉，逮捕勃治之。

translations.literal: Afterward someone submitted a memorial accusing Bo of wishing to rebel; he was handed to the commandant of justice, arrested Bo, and tried him.
translations.idiomatic: Then a denunciation claimed Zhou Bo meant to revolt; the case went to the commandant of justice and he was jailed.

sentences.zh: 勃恐，不知置辭。

translations.literal: Bo was afraid and did not know how to phrase his reply.
translations.idiomatic: He was too frightened to frame a defense.

sentences.zh: 吏稍侵辱之。

translations.literal: The clerks gradually insulted and humiliated him.
translations.idiomatic: The jailors bullied him daily.

sentences.zh: 勃以千金與獄吏，獄吏乃書牘背示之，曰「以公主為證」。

translations.literal: Bo gave a thousand jin to the jail clerk; the jail clerk then wrote on the back of a slip and showed him, saying "use the princess as witness."
translations.idiomatic: He slipped a thousand jin to the head jailer, who scribbled on a wooden slip: "Call in the princess."

sentences.zh: 公主者，孝文帝女也，勃太子勝之尚之，故獄吏教引為證。

translations.literal: The princess is Emperor Xiaowen's daughter; Bo's heir apparent Shengzhi had married her; therefore the jail clerk taught him to cite her as witness.
translations.idiomatic: The princess was Emperor Wen's daughter, wife to Zhou Bo's heir—hence the hint to summon her as alibi.

sentences.zh: 初，勃之益封，盡以予薄昭。

translations.literal: At first, when Bo's fief was increased, he gave it all to Bo Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Years before, Zhou Bo had heaped his bonus lands on his brother-in-law Bo Zhao.

sentences.zh: 及繫急，薄昭為言薄太后，太后亦以為無反事。

translations.literal: When the bonds grew urgent, Bo Zhao spoke for him to Empress Dowager Bo; the empress dowager also thought there was no rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Now Bo Zhao pleaded with Empress Dowager Bo, who was convinced of Zhou Bo's innocence.

sentences.zh: 文帝朝，太后以冒絮提文帝，曰：「絳侯綰皇帝璽，將兵於北軍，不以此時反，今居一小縣，顧欲反邪！」

translations.literal: At Emperor Wen's court session the empress dowager took a padded head-cloth and struck Emperor Wen, saying, "The Marquis of Jiang held the imperial seal, led troops at the Northern Army—if he did not rebel then, now dwelling in one small county, could he wish to rebel!"
translations.idiomatic: At court she struck her son with a veil knot and scolded, "Zhou Bo held the seal and the northern legions—if he meant treason he had his chance then; now, retired to a tiny fief, would he rebel?"

sentences.zh: 文帝既見勃獄辭，乃謝曰：「吏方驗而出之。」

translations.literal: After Emperor Wen had seen Bo's prison statement, he apologized, saying, "The clerks are just now verifying and will release him."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen read the deposition and muttered, "The clerks are still checking—they will let him go."

sentences.zh: 於是使使持節赦勃，復爵邑。

translations.literal: Thereupon he sent an envoy bearing credentials to pardon Bo and restore his noble rank and estates.
translations.idiomatic: He sent a courier with the tally of pardon and restored Zhou Bo's title and lands.

sentences.zh: 勃既出，曰：「吾嘗將百萬軍，安知獄吏之貴也！」

translations.literal: After Bo came out he said, "I once commanded a million troops—how could I have known the jail clerk was so exalted!"
translations.idiomatic: Walking free, Zhou Bo said, "I commanded a million men—who knew a jailer could hold my life in his hand?"

sentences.zh: 勃復就國，孝文十一年薨，諡曰武侯。

translations.literal: Bo again went to his fief; in the eleventh year of Emperor Xiaowen he died; his posthumous title was Marquis Wu.
translations.idiomatic: He retired to his fief and died in Emperor Wen's eleventh year with the posthumous name "Marquis Wu" (Martial).

sentences.zh: 子勝之嗣，尚公主不相中，坐殺人，死，國絕。

translations.literal: His son Shengzhi inherited; he did not get along with the princess he had married; he sat guilty of killing a man, died, and the state was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: His son Shengzhi inherited but quarreled with the princess, killed a man, and died—ending the line.

sentences.zh: 一年，弟亞夫復為侯。

translations.literal: After one year the younger brother Yafu again became marquis.
translations.idiomatic: A year later the younger brother Zhou Yafu was named marquis again.

sentences.zh: 亞夫為河內守時，許負相之：「君後三歲而侯。

translations.literal: When Yafu was governor of Hedong, Xu Fu read his physiognomy: "Sir, after three years you will be a marquis.
translations.idiomatic: While Zhou Yafu governed Hedong, the physiognomist Xu Fu told him, "In three years you will hold a marquisate.

sentences.zh: 侯八歲，為將相，持國秉，貴重矣，於人臣無二。

translations.literal: For eight years as marquis you will be general and chancellor, holding the state's authority—heavy and honored; among ministers there will be no second.
translations.idiomatic: Eight years as marquis will bring you to general and chancellor—second to none among ministers.

sentences.zh: 後九年而餓死。」

translations.literal: After nine more years you will starve to death."
translations.idiomatic: Nine years after that you will die of hunger."

sentences.zh: 亞夫笑曰：「臣之兄以代父侯矣，有如卒，子當代，我何說侯乎？

translations.literal: Yafu laughed and said, "My elder brother already succeeds our father as marquis; if it happens that he dies, the son should succeed—what talk is there of my being marquis?
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu laughed: "My brother already holds our father's title; if he dies his son inherits—how should I become marquis?

sentences.zh: 然既已貴如負言，又何說餓死？

translations.literal: Yet since it is already as noble as Fu said, what talk is there of starving to death?
translations.idiomatic: And if I rose so high, how could I starve?

sentences.zh: 指視我。」

translations.literal: Point and show me."
translations.idiomatic: Show me the signs."

sentences.zh: 負指其口曰：「從理入口，此餓死法也。」

translations.literal: Fu pointed at his mouth and said, "The vertical crease entering the mouth—this is the pattern of starving to death."
translations.idiomatic: Xu Fu traced the vertical furrow down into his lips and said, "That channel is the mark of death by famine."

sentences.zh: 居三歲，兄絳侯勝之有罪，文帝擇勃子賢者，皆推亞夫，乃封為條侯。

translations.literal: After three years his elder brother Marquis of Jiang Shengzhi had a crime; Emperor Wen chose among Bo's sons for the worthy one; all pushed Yafu; then he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Tiao.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later Shengzhi fell from grace; Emperor Wen asked which of Zhou Bo's sons was worthiest—the court named Yafu, who received the marquisate of Tiao.

sentences.zh: 文帝後六年，匈奴大入邊。

translations.literal: In the sixth year after Emperor Wen's accession, the Xiongnu invaded the border in force.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of Emperor Wen's reign the Xiongnu raided deep into the frontier.

sentences.zh: 以宗正劉禮為將軍軍霸上，祝茲侯徐厲為將軍軍棘門，以河內守亞夫為將軍軍細柳，以備胡。

translations.literal: He made Director of the Imperial Clan Liu Li general garrisoning Bashang, Marquis of Zhuzi Xu Li general garrisoning Jimen, and Hedong governor Yafu general garrisoning Xiliu, to guard against the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: He posted Liu Li at Bashang, Xu Li at Jimen, and Zhou Yafu at Xiliu to block the nomads.

sentences.zh: 上自勞軍，至霸上及棘門軍，直馳入，將以下騎出入送迎。

translations.literal: The emperor personally inspected the troops; arriving at the Bashang and Jimen armies, he drove straight in; from general downward they rode out to send off and welcome.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor toured the camps: at Bashang and Jimen his chariot raced through unchallenged while officers rode out to salute him.

sentences.zh: 已而之細柳軍，軍士吏被甲，銳兵刃，彀弓弩，持滿。

translations.literal: Presently he went to the Xiliu army; officers and soldiers wore armor, sharp weapons, bent crossbows drawn full.
translations.idiomatic: At Xiliu every man stood in mail with blades bare and crossbows cocked.

sentences.zh: 天子先驅至，不得入。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven's vanguard arrived and could not enter.
translations.idiomatic: Even the imperial vanguard was stopped at the gate.

sentences.zh: 先驅曰：「天子且至！」

translations.literal: The vanguard said, "The Son of Heaven is about to arrive!"
translations.idiomatic: The escort shouted that the emperor was coming.

sentences.zh: 軍門都尉曰：「軍中聞將軍之令，不聞天子之詔。」

translations.literal: The commandant of the camp gate said, "Within the army one hears the general's orders, not the Son of Heaven's edicts."
translations.idiomatic: The gate captain answered, "In camp we obey the general, not the throne."

sentences.zh: 有頃，上至，又不得入。

translations.literal: After a while the emperor arrived and again could not enter.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor himself arrived he was still barred.

sentences.zh: 於是上使使持節詔將軍曰：「吾欲勞軍。」

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor sent an envoy bearing credentials to announce to the general, "I wish to inspect the troops."
translations.idiomatic: He sent a messenger with the imperial tally: "I come to review the army."

sentences.zh: 亞夫乃傳言開壁門。

translations.literal: Yafu then passed word to open the camp gate.
translations.idiomatic: Only then did Zhou Yafu order the gates opened.

sentences.zh: 壁門士請車騎曰：「將軍約，軍中不得驅馳。」

translations.literal: The camp-gate soldiers requested of the chariots and cavalry, "The general's rule: within the army one must not gallop."
translations.idiomatic: The pickets told the escort, "No galloping inside these lines."

sentences.zh: 於是天子乃按轡徐行。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven reined in and went slowly.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor touched his reins and crawled forward at a walk.

sentences.zh: 至中營，將軍亞夫揖，曰：「介冑之士不拜，請以軍禮見。」

translations.literal: Reaching the middle camp, General Yafu made a military salute and said, "Men in armor do not bow; I beg to be seen with military rites."
translations.idiomatic: In the inner camp Zhou Yafu saluted at arms: "Armored men do not kneel—I greet you by military courtesy."

sentences.zh: 天子為動，改容式車。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven was moved, changed countenance, and performed the vehicle salute on the chariot rail.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen straightened on his chariot and raised his hand to the crossbar in salute.

sentences.zh: 使人稱謝：「皇帝敬勞將軍。」

translations.literal: He sent men to express thanks: "The emperor respectfully thanks the general."
translations.idiomatic: He sent word: "The emperor bows to the general."

sentences.zh: 成禮而去。

translations.literal: When the rites were completed he departed.
translations.idiomatic: The review ended and the court party withdrew.

sentences.zh: 既出軍門，群臣皆驚。

translations.literal: After exiting the army gate, the assembled ministers were all astonished.
translations.idiomatic: Outside the camp the ministers gaped.

sentences.zh: 文帝曰：「嗟乎，此真將軍矣！

translations.literal: Emperor Wen said, "Alas, this is a true general!"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen sighed, "There is a real general at last!"

sentences.zh: 鄉者霸上、棘門如兒戲耳，其將固可襲而虜也。

translations.literal: Just now Bashang and Jimen were like children's play; their generals indeed could be raided and captured.
translations.idiomatic: Bashang and Jimen were a sham—any raider could have walked in and seized those generals.

sentences.zh: 至於亞夫，可得而犯邪！」

translations.literal: As for Yafu—can he be violated!"
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu's camp—no enemy could touch that."

sentences.zh: 稱善者久之。

translations.literal: He praised it as good for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: He went on praising the scene long after the chariots had gone.

sentences.zh: 月餘，三軍皆罷。

translations.literal: After more than a month the three armies were all dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Within a month the emergency levies were stood down.

sentences.zh: 乃拜亞夫為中尉。

translations.literal: Then he appointed Yafu commandant of the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He named Zhou Yafu commandant of the capital guards.

sentences.zh: 文帝且崩時，戒太子曰：「即有緩急，周亞夫真可任將兵。」

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen was about to die he admonished the heir, saying, "If there should be crisis or calm, Zhou Yafu is truly fit to be entrusted with command of troops."
translations.idiomatic: On his deathbed Emperor Wen told the crown prince, "In any crisis put the army in Zhou Yafu's hands."

sentences.zh: 文帝崩，亞夫為車騎將軍。
