translations.literal: When Emperor Wen died, Yafu became chariot-and-cavalry general.
translations.idiomatic: After Wen's death Zhou Yafu became chariot and cavalry general.

sentences.zh: 孝景帝三年，吳楚反。

translations.literal: In the third year of Emperor Xiaojing, Wu and Chu rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's third year the kingdoms of Wu and Chu rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 亞夫以中尉為太尉，東擊吳楚。

translations.literal: Yafu from commandant of the capital became grand commandant and struck east against Wu and Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted from capital commandant to grand commandant, he marched east against the rebels.

sentences.zh: 因自請上曰：「楚兵剽輕，難與爭鋒。

translations.literal: He therefore personally requested of the emperor, saying, "Chu's troops are swift and light—difficult to contend with edge on edge.
translations.idiomatic: He warned the throne: "Chu's host is fast and mobile—do not meet it head-on.

sentences.zh: 願以梁委之，絕其食道，乃可制也。」

translations.literal: I beg to throw Liang to them, cut their supply road, and then they can be controlled."
translations.idiomatic: Let Liang absorb their fury while I strangle their supply lines—that is how we break them."

sentences.zh: 上許之。

translations.literal: The emperor granted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed.

sentences.zh: 亞夫既發，至霸上，趙涉遮說亞夫曰：「將軍東誅吳楚，勝則宗廟安，不勝則天下危，能用臣之言乎？」

translations.literal: After Yafu had set out, when he reached Bashang, Zhao She blocked the way and urged Yafu, saying, "General, you go east to punish Wu and Chu—if you win the ancestral temples are safe; if you lose the realm is in peril—can you use your servant's words?"
translations.idiomatic: At Bashang a commoner named Zhao She stopped his chariot: "General, this campaign decides whether the altars stand or fall—will you hear me out?"

sentences.zh: 亞夫下車，禮而問之。

translations.literal: Yafu dismounted from the chariot, treated him courteously, and asked him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu stepped down and listened with respect.

sentences.zh: 涉曰：「吳王素富，懷輯死士久矣。

translations.literal: She said, "The King of Wu has long been rich and for a long time has cherished and gathered bravos ready to die.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao She said, "The king of Wu is rich; he has long hoarded assassins for this day.

sentences.zh: 此知將軍且行，必置間人於殽黽阨骥之間。

translations.literal: They know the general is about to march and will surely place spies in the narrow passes between Yao and Min.
translations.idiomatic: He knows your route and will plant men in the Yao–Min defiles.

sentences.zh: 且兵事上神密，將軍何不從此右去，走藍田，出武關，抵雒陽，間不過差一二日，直入武庫，擊鳴鼓。

translations.literal: Moreover military affairs value secrecy above all—why does the general not go rightward from here, take the road through Lantian, exit Wuguan, reach Luoyang—only a day or two difference—and go straight into the armory, strike the signal drum?
translations.idiomatic: Take the secret road: bear south through Lantian, burst out at Wuguan, hit Luoyang a day behind schedule, seize the imperial armory, and beat the war drum.

sentences.zh: 諸侯聞之，以為將軍從天而下也。」

translations.literal: When the lords hear it they will think the general descended from heaven."
translations.idiomatic: The rebels will think you fell from the sky."

sentences.zh: 太尉如其計。

translations.literal: The grand commandant followed his plan.
translations.idiomatic: The grand commandant followed Zhao She's advice to the letter.

sentences.zh: 至雒陽，使吏搜殽黽間，果得吳伏兵。

translations.literal: When he reached Luoyang he ordered clerks to search the space between Yao and Min and indeed obtained Wu's ambush troops.
translations.idiomatic: In Luoyang he sent scouts into the Yao–Min passes and flushed out Wu's ambush.

sentences.zh: 乃請涉為護軍。

translations.literal: He then requested She to be guard commander.
translations.idiomatic: He enlisted Zhao She as his chief of escort.

sentences.zh: 亞夫至，會兵滎陽。

translations.literal: When Yafu arrived he joined forces at Yingyang.
translations.idiomatic: He consolidated the host at Yingyang.

sentences.zh: 吳方攻梁，梁急，請救。

translations.literal: Wu was then attacking Liang; Liang was urgent and requested rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Wu was hammering Liang, which begged for relief.

sentences.zh: 亞夫引兵東北走昌邑，深壁而守。

translations.literal: Yafu led troops northeast to Changyi, dug in deep behind high walls, and held fast.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu slipped northeast to Changyi and refused battle behind strong ramparts.

sentences.zh: 梁王使使請亞夫，亞夫守便宜，不往。

translations.literal: The King of Liang sent envoys to request Yafu; Yafu guarded what was expedient and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Liu Wu of Liang sent courier after courier; Zhou Yafu stuck to his plan and stayed put.

sentences.zh: 梁上書言景帝，景帝詔使救梁。

translations.literal: Liang submitted a memorial stating it to Emperor Jing; Emperor Jing issued an edict ordering him to save Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Liang appealed to Emperor Jing, who issued an edict ordering Han relief.

sentences.zh: 亞夫不奉詔，堅壁不出，而使輕騎兵弓高侯等絕吳楚兵後食道。

translations.literal: Yafu did not obey the edict, held fast behind the walls and did not go out, but sent light cavalry such as Marquis Gonggao to cut Wu and Chu's rear supply road.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu ignored the imperial courier and kept his walls shut while light horse under the Marquis of Gonggao severed the rebel supply line.

sentences.zh: 吳楚兵乏糧，飢，欲退，數挑戰，終不出。

translations.literal: Wu and Chu's troops lacked grain, hungered, wished to withdraw, repeatedly challenged battle, but in the end he did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: The allies starved, taunted his lines, and tried to retreat—he never offered battle.

sentences.zh: 夜，軍中驚，內相攻擊擾亂，至於帳下。

translations.literal: At night the army was alarmed; internally they attacked each other in confusion, reaching as far as the commander's tent.
translations.idiomatic: One night his own camp panicked and men struck one another almost to his headquarters.

sentences.zh: 亞夫堅臥不起。

translations.literal: Yafu lay firm and did not rise.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu stayed in bed and never stirred.

sentences.zh: 頃之，復定。

translations.literal: After a while it was calm again.
translations.idiomatic: Order returned without his leaving the couch.

sentences.zh: 吳奔壁東南陬，亞夫使備西北。

translations.literal: Wu rushed the wall's southeast corner; Yafu had the northwest prepared.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu feinted at the southeast corner of his camp, Zhou Yafu massed his reserves in the northwest.

sentences.zh: 已而其精兵果奔西北，不得入。

translations.literal: Presently their crack troops indeed rushed the northwest but could not enter.
translations.idiomatic: The enemy's best troops swung northwest as he had foreseen and were thrown back.

sentences.zh: 吳楚既餓，乃引而去。

translations.literal: When Wu and Chu were already starving, they then led away and left.
translations.idiomatic: Starved out, Wu and Chu broke camp and fled.

sentences.zh: 亞夫出精兵追擊，大破吳王濞。

translations.literal: Yafu led crack troops in pursuit and greatly defeated King of Wu Pi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu hurled his veterans after them and shattered Liu Pi's army.

sentences.zh: 吳王濞棄其軍，與壯士數千人亡走，保於江南丹徒。

translations.literal: King of Wu Pi abandoned his army and with several thousand stalwarts fled in hiding, holding out south of the river at Dantu.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Pi deserted his host and raced to Dantu south of the Yangzi with a few thousand bodyguards.

sentences.zh: 漢兵因乘勝，遂盡虜之，降其縣，購吳王千金。

translations.literal: Han troops therefore rode the victory; thereupon they fully captured them, reduced their counties, and offered a thousand jin of gold for the King of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Han forces mopped up the towns, offered a thousand-jin bounty for Liu Pi's head, and accepted surrenders.

sentences.zh: 月餘，越人斬吳王頭以告。

translations.literal: After more than a month Yue men beheaded the King of Wu and reported with the head.
translations.idiomatic: A month later Yue irregulars sent in Liu Pi's head.

sentences.zh: 凡相守攻三月，而吳楚破平。

translations.literal: In all they besieged and attacked each other three months, and Wu and Chu were broken and pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Three months of siege broke the rebellion.

sentences.zh: 於是諸將乃以太尉計謀為是。

translations.literal: Thereupon the generals then deemed the grand commandant's stratagem right.
translations.idiomatic: Only then did his captains admit his strategy had been sound.

sentences.zh: 由此梁孝王與亞夫有隙。

translations.literal: From this King Xiao of Liang and Yafu had a rift.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Liu Wu of Liang never forgave the slight.

sentences.zh: 歸，復置太尉官。

translations.literal: On returning, he again established the office of grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: After victory the court restored the grand commandant's office.

sentences.zh: 五歲，遷為丞相，景帝甚重之。

translations.literal: After five years he was promoted to chancellor; Emperor Jing greatly esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later Zhou Yafu became chancellor, high in Jing's favor.

sentences.zh: 上廢栗太子，亞夫固爭之，不待。

translations.literal: The emperor deposed Crown Prince Li; Yafu firmly disputed it but was not heeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Jing cast aside the heir born of Lady Li, Zhou Yafu fought the decision—and was ignored.

sentences.zh: 上由此疏之。

translations.literal: The emperor for this reason grew distant from him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor cooled toward him from that day.

sentences.zh: 而梁孝王每朝，常與太后言亞夫之短。

translations.literal: And King Xiao of Liang, whenever he attended court, always spoke to the empress dowager of Yafu's faults.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Liu Wu whispered slander to his mother whenever he came to court.

sentences.zh: 竇太后曰：「皇后兄王信可侯也。」

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou said, "The empress's elder brother Wang Xin may be enfeoffed as marquis."
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dou demanded a marquisate for her daughter-in-law's brother Wang Xin.

sentences.zh: 上讓曰：「始南皮及章武先帝不侯，及臣即位，乃侯之，信未得封也。」

translations.literal: The emperor demurred, saying, "At first Nanpi and Zhangwu the late emperor did not enfeoff as marquises; only when your servant mounted the throne were they enfeoffed—Xin has not yet earned a patent."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing objected that his late father had never enfeoffed the Dou brothers during his lifetime—Wang Xin had done nothing to deserve a title.

sentences.zh: 竇太后曰：「人生各以時行耳。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou said, "In life each person acts in his own season.
translations.idiomatic: "Everyone gets a turn," she snapped.

sentences.zh: 竇長君在時，竟不得封侯，死後，乃其子彭祖顧得侯。

translations.literal: When the elder lord of Dou was alive, in the end he could not obtain enfeoffment as marquis; after he died, only then did his son Pengzu contrary to expectation obtain a marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: Her brother Dou Zhang had died without a fief; only his son Pengzu had been titled.

sentences.zh: 吾甚恨之。

translations.literal: I greatly resent it.
translations.idiomatic: That still rankled her.

sentences.zh: 帝趣侯信也！」

translations.literal: Emperor, hurry and enfeoff Xin as marquis!"
translations.idiomatic: So ennoble Wang Xin at once!"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「請得與丞相計之。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "I beg to consult with the chancellor."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said he must consult his chancellor.

sentences.zh: 亞夫曰：「高帝約『非劉氏不得王，非有功不得侯。

translations.literal: Yafu said, "The High Emperor covenanted: 'Whoever is not of the Liu clan may not be king; whoever lacks merit may not be marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu quoted the white-horse oath: no non-Liu kings, no unearned marquisates.

sentences.zh: 不如約，天下共擊之』。

translations.literal: Whoever breaks the covenant—the world shall strike him together.'
translations.idiomatic: Break that rule and the realm rises against you."

sentences.zh: 今信雖皇后兄，無功，侯之，非約也。」

translations.literal: Now although Xin is the empress's elder brother, without merit, to enfeoff him breaks the covenant."
translations.idiomatic: Wang Xin is the empress's brother but has won no battle—ennobling him would violate the oath."

sentences.zh: 上默然而沮。

translations.literal: The emperor was silent and dejected.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing fell silent, beaten.

sentences.zh: 其後匈奴王徐盧等五人降漢，上欲侯之以勸後。

translations.literal: Afterward when the Xiongnu kings Xu Lu and five others surrendered to Han, the emperor wished to enfeoff them as marquises to encourage those who came later.
translations.idiomatic: Later five Xiongnu chiefs including Xu Lu defected; Emperor Jing meant to enfeoff them as a signal to others.

sentences.zh: 亞夫曰：「彼背其主降陛下，陛下侯之，即何以責人臣不守節者乎？」

translations.literal: Yafu said, "They betrayed their lords to surrender to Your Majesty; if Your Majesty enfeoffs them, with what will you reprove ministers who do not keep integrity?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu protested: "Reward turncoats today and how do you punish tomorrow's traitors?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「丞相議不可用。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "The chancellor's deliberation cannot be used."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said flatly, "The chancellor is overruled."

sentences.zh: 乃悉封徐盧等為列侯。

translations.literal: Thereupon he fully enfeoffed Xu Lu and the others as full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed every one of the defectors anyway.

sentences.zh: 亞夫因謝病免相。

translations.literal: Yafu thereupon declined on grounds of illness and was relieved of chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 頃之，上居禁中，召亞夫賜食。

translations.literal: After a while the emperor dwelt in the forbidden quarters and summoned Yafu to grant food.
translations.idiomatic: Soon afterward Emperor Jing summoned him to a private meal in the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 獨置大胾，無切肉，又不置箸。

translations.literal: He alone set out a great chunk of meat, no sliced meat, and also did not set out chopsticks.
translations.idiomatic: The table held only a huge roast joint—no slices, no chopsticks.

sentences.zh: 亞夫心不平，顧謂尚席取箸。

translations.literal: Yafu's heart was unbalanced; he turned and told the overseer of the mat to fetch chopsticks.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu scowled and called for an attendant to bring chopsticks.

sentences.zh: 上視而笑曰：「此非不足君所乎？」

translations.literal: The emperor looked and laughed, saying, "Is this not insufficient for your requirements?"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing watched and laughed: "Surely this spread does not suit you?"

sentences.zh: 亞夫免冠謝上。

translations.literal: Yafu removed his cap and apologized to the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu doffed his cap and apologized.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「起。」

translations.literal: The emperor said, "Rise."
translations.idiomatic: "Rise," said the emperor.

sentences.zh: 亞夫因趨出。

translations.literal: Yafu thereupon hurried out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu withdrew in haste.

sentences.zh: 上目送之，曰：「此鞅鞅，非少主臣也！」

translations.literal: The emperor watched him with his eyes until he was gone and said, "This man is disgruntled—no minister for a young sovereign!"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing watched him go and muttered, "That sullen pride will never serve my heir."

sentences.zh: 居無何，亞夫子為父買工官尚方甲楯五百被可以葬者。

translations.literal: Before long Yafu's son, for his father, bought five hundred sets of armor and shields from the imperial workshop foreman fit for burial equipment.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu's son ordered five hundred suits of funeral armor from the imperial foundry for his father's tomb.

sentences.zh: 取庸苦之，不與錢。

translations.literal: He took hired laborers and worked them harshly, not giving them money.
translations.idiomatic: He worked the carriers to the bone and refused to pay them.

sentences.zh: 庸知其盜買縣官器，怨而上變告子，事連汙亞夫。

translations.literal: The laborers knew he had stolen-bought the government's vessels, resented it, and submitted a report of emergency accusing the son; the affair implicated and stained Yafu.
translations.idiomatic: They knew the gear was government property and filed a treason charge that snared the old general.

sentences.zh: 書既聞，上下吏。

translations.literal: When the memorial had been heard, it was handed down to clerks.
translations.idiomatic: The throne forwarded the denunciation to the judiciary.

sentences.zh: 吏簿責亞夫，亞夫不對。

translations.literal: The clerks interrogated from the register and pressed Yafu; Yafu did not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Clerks badgered Zhou Yafu; he refused to speak.

sentences.zh: 上罵之曰：「吾不用也。」

translations.literal: The emperor cursed him, saying, "I will not use him."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing snarled that he would not lift a finger for him.

sentences.zh: 召詣廷尉。

translations.literal: He was summoned to the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: He was hauled before the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 廷尉責問曰：「君侯欲反何？」

translations.literal: The commandant of justice pressed him with questions, saying, "Lord marquis, why do you wish to rebel?"
translations.idiomatic: The judge demanded, "Why do you mean to rebel?"

sentences.zh: 亞夫曰：「臣所買器，乃葬器也，何謂反乎？」

translations.literal: Yafu said, "The vessels your servant bought are burial equipment—what is called rebellion in that?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu answered, "Those are funeral arms for the tomb—what treason is there?"

sentences.zh: 吏曰：「君縱不欲反地上，即欲反地下耳。」

translations.literal: The clerk said, "Even if you do not wish to rebel on earth, you mean to rebel under the earth."
translations.idiomatic: The clerk sneered, "So you rebel in the next world instead."

sentences.zh: 吏侵之益急。

translations.literal: The clerks pressed him ever more urgently.
translations.idiomatic: The interrogation turned brutal.

sentences.zh: 初，吏捕亞夫，亞夫欲自殺，其夫人止之，以故不得死，遂入廷尉，因不食五日，歐血而死。

translations.literal: At first when the clerks arrested Yafu, Yafu wished to kill himself; his wife stopped him; for this reason he did not obtain death, then entered the commandant of justice, and because he did not eat for five days, vomited blood and died.
translations.idiomatic: At first he tried suicide; his wife stopped him. In prison he fasted five days until blood burst from his mouth and he died.

sentences.zh: 國絕。

translations.literal: The state was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The marquisate ended.

sentences.zh: 一歲，上乃更封絳侯勃它子堅為平曲侯，續絳侯後。

translations.literal: After one year the emperor then changed the enfeoffment and made another of Marquis of Jiang Bo's sons, Jian, Marquis of Pingqu, continuing the line of Marquis of Jiang.
translations.idiomatic: A year later Emperor Jing enfeoffed another son of Zhou Bo, Zhou Jian, as Marquis of Pingqu to continue the Jiang line.

sentences.zh: 傳子建德，為太子太傅，坐酎金免官。

translations.literal: It passed to son Jiande, who was grand tutor to the crown prince and sat guilty of inferior sacrificial gold and was removed from office.
translations.idiomatic: The title passed to Zhou Jiande, grand tutor to the crown prince, who lost office over thin ritual wine.

sentences.zh: 後有罪，國除。

translations.literal: Afterward he had a crime; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Further offenses wiped out the fief.

sentences.zh: 亞夫果餓死。

translations.literal: Yafu indeed starved to death.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yafu died of hunger as Xu Fu had foretold.

sentences.zh: 死後，上乃封王信為蓋侯。

translations.literal: After he died, the emperor then enfeoffed Wang Xin as Marquis of Gai.
translations.idiomatic: Only after Zhou Yafu's death did Emperor Jing make Wang Xin marquis of Gai.

sentences.zh: 至平帝元始二年，繼絕世，復封勃玄孫之子恭為絳侯，千戶。

translations.literal: By the second year of Emperor Ping's Yuanshi era, continuing severed lines, he again enfeoffed the son of Bo's great-great-grandson, Gong, as Marquis of Jiang, one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Ping's second year the court revived the Zhou line, naming Gong, a descendant of Zhou Bo, marquis of Jiang at one thousand households.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：聞張良之智勇，以為其貌魁梧奇偉，反若婦人女子。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: Hearing of Zhang Liang's wisdom and courage, one thinks his appearance must be towering and extraordinarily grand—on the contrary he was like a woman or girl.
translations.idiomatic: The historian's verdict: you expect Zhang Liang to look like a hero; he looked like a scholar or a woman.

sentences.zh: 故孔子稱「以貌取人，失之子羽」。

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius praised, "Judging people by appearance, one misses Ziyu."
translations.idiomatic: Hence Confucius warned that faces deceive—Ziyu taught that lesson.

sentences.zh: 學者多疑於鬼神，如良受書老父，亦異矣。

translations.literal: Scholars often doubt ghosts and spirits; as for Liang receiving a book from an old man on the bridge—that too is strange.
translations.idiomatic: Rationalists scoff at spirits, yet Zhang Liang took his warcraft from a greybeard on a bridge.

sentences.zh: 高祖數離困阨，良常有力，豈可謂非天乎！

translations.literal: The High Ancestor repeatedly escaped peril; Liang often had power therein—how can it be said not to be heaven!
translations.idiomatic: Time after time Liu Bang slipped the noose with Zhang Liang's aid—call it fate if you will.

sentences.zh: 陳平之志，見於社下，傾側擾攘楚、魏之間，卒歸於漢，而為謀臣。

translations.literal: Chen Ping's ambition showed below the village altar; he tilted and stirred between Chu and Wei, in the end returned to Han, and became a planning minister.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping announced his hunger for power at a village sacrifice, drifted between Wei and Chu, and ended as Han's master intriguer.

sentences.zh: 及呂后時，事多故矣，平竟自免，以智終。

translations.literal: By Empress Lü's time affairs had many changes; Ping in the end freed himself and ended by wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: Under Empress Lü he navigated the shoals and retired with his wits intact.

sentences.zh: 王陵廷爭，杜門自絕，亦各其志也。

translations.literal: Wang Ling disputed at court, shut his door and cut himself off—each also had his aspiration.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling stormed the court, then locked his gate—each man followed his conscience.

sentences.zh: 周勃為布衣時，鄙樸庸人，至登輔佐，匡國家難，誅諸呂，立孝文，為漢伊周，何其盛也！

translations.literal: When Zhou Bo was in common cloth he was a rustic plain man; reaching the point of ascending to assistant rule, rectifying the state in difficulty, executing the various Lüs, establishing Emperor Xiaowen—he was Han's Yi and Zhou—how grand!
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo was a peasant who became the dynasty's pillar: he purged the Lüs and enthroned Emperor Wen—Han's answer to the ancient ministers Yi Yin and the Duke of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 始呂后問宰相，高祖曰：「陳平智有餘，王陵少戇，可以佐之；

translations.literal: When Empress Lü first asked about the chancellors, the High Ancestor said, "Chen Ping's wisdom is more than enough; Wang Ling is somewhat simple—he can assist him;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bang once told his wife: "Chen Ping has brains to spare; Wang Ling is a little blunt—let him assist Chen Ping.

sentences.zh: 安劉氏者必勃也。」

translations.literal: The one who will secure the Liu house must be Bo."
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo is the man who will save the Liu line."

sentences.zh: 又問其次，云「過此以後，非乃所及」。

translations.literal: He was asked about the next rank; he said, "Beyond this, it is not what I can reach."
translations.idiomatic: Asked who came next, he said, "After that I cannot see."

sentences.zh: 終皆如言，聖矣夫！

translations.literal: In the end all came to pass as he had said—how sagely indeed!
translations.idiomatic: Every judgment the High Emperor passed on these men proved true in the end—what sage insight that was!
